signals.raw:
**Raptors' struggles**: Toronto committed a season-high 22 turnovers despite scoring 16 fast-break points. Scottie Barnes scored a playoff career-high 26 points, but Brandon Ingram struggled significantly with just 7 points on 3-for-15 shooting.[1]

With the Cavaliers holding a 2-0 series advantage and showing dominant performances from their star players, they are in a strong position to advance.

signals.headline: Cavaliers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Raptors with Dominant Game 2 Victory | CAVALIERS FAVORED | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Cavaliers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Raptors with Dominant Game 2 Victory | CAVALIERS FAVORED | HIGH confidence**

The **Cleveland Cavaliers defeated the Toronto Raptors 115-105 in Game 2** on Monday night, taking a commanding 2-0 lead in their first-round Eastern Conference playoff series.[1] This result strongly favors the Cavaliers to win the series.
matched_markets: NBA Playoffs: Who Will Win Series? - Raptors vs. Cavaliers

signals.headline: **Star trio dominance**: Donovan Mitchell (30 points), James Harden (28 points), and Evan Mobley (25 points) combined fo
signals.raw: **Star trio dominance**: Donovan Mitchell (30 points), James Harden (28 points), and Evan Mobley (25 points) combined for 83 points, marking the fourth time in franchise history the Cavs have had three players score 25+ points in a playoff game.[1]

signals.raw: **Historic streak**: Cleveland extended its playoff winning streak against Toronto to 12 consecutive victories, matching an NBA postseason record for consecutive wins against an opponent.[1]
